pattern: Because any palindrome of length at least k contains a centered palindrome of length k or k+1, a greedy left-to-right scan that closes the earliest valid palindrome and jumps past it counts the maximum non-overlapping selections.
hints: A long palindrome always hides a shorter centered palindrome of length k or k+1, so you never need substrings longer than k+1.
Scanning positions left to right, finish a palindrome as soon as you can and then skip past its end to keep selections disjoint.
Expand around each center; whenever the palindrome length first reaches k, take it, advance the cursor to just after its right end, and increment the count.

edge_inputs.description: no palindrome reaches the minimum length
every character is its own palindrome when k is one
one long palindrome counts once, greedily split for more
overlapping candidates must be chosen disjointly

stress.description: random small-alphabet strings to create many overlapping palindromes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, 4)
    return [s, k]
